2603.20636.title: A Modular LLM Framework for Explainable Price Outlier Detection
2603.20636.paper_summary: Detecting product price outliers is important for retail and e-commerce stores as erroneous or unexpectedly high prices adversely affect competitiveness, revenue, and consumer trust. Classical techniques offer simple thresholds while ignoring the rich semantic relationships among product attributes. We propose an agentic Large Language Model (LLM) framework that treats outlier price flagging as a reasoning task grounded in related product detection and comparison. The system processes the prices of target products in three stages: (i) relevance classification selects price-relevant similar products using product descriptions and attributes; (ii) relative utility assessment evaluates the target product against each similar product along price influencing dimensions (e.g., brand, size, features); (iii) reasoning-based decision aggregates these justifications into an explainable price outlier judgment. The framework attains over 75% agreement with human auditors on a test dataset, and outperforms zero-shot and retrieval based LLM techniques. Ablation studies show the sensitivity of the method to key hyper-parameters and testify on its flexibility to be applied to cases with different accuracy requirement and auditor agreements.
2603.20636.paper_summary_zh: 偵測產品價格異常對於零售和電子商務商店而言非常重要，因為錯誤或意外高的價格會對競爭力、收入和消費者信任產生不利影響。傳統技術提供簡單的閾值，但忽略了產品屬性之間豐富的語義關係。我們提出了一個代理性的大型語言模型（LLM）框架，將異常價格標記視為一個基於相關產品檢測和比較的推理任務。該系統以三個階段處理目標產品的價格：（i）相關性分類使用產品描述和屬性選擇價格相關的相似產品；（ii）相對效用評估根據價格影響維度（例如，品牌、大小、特徵）評估目標產品與每個相似產品的比較；（iii）基於推理的決策將這些理由聚合成可解釋的價格異常判斷。該框架在測試數據集上與人類審核者達成超過75%的一致，並超越了零樣本和基於檢索的LLM技術。消融研究顯示該方法對關鍵超參數的敏感性，並證明其在不同準確性要求和審核者一致性案例中的靈活應用。
2603.20636.author: Shadi Sartipi et.al.
2603.20636.authors: Shadi Sartipi, John Wu, Sina Ghotbi, Nikhita Vedula, Shervin Malmasi
